dsl_rule.translation_notes: 52-week high approximated using Donchian Channel upper band (dc_upper) with 52-week lookback period. Volume condition translated using vol_ratio_20 (volume ratio to 20-day average). Exit on volume dryup would require multi-bar sequential logic not expressible in this schema, so simplified to primary exit condition. Max adverse excursion condition also not directly expressible; stop_loss_pct of 2.0 captures the explicit stop level. Signal is LONG-only based on bullish anchoring exploitation.

backtest_rule.reason: 52-week high approximated using Donchian Channel upper band (dc_upper) with 52-week lookback period. Volume condition translated using vol_ratio_20 (volume ratio to 20-day average). Exit on volume dryup would require multi-bar sequential logic not expressible in this schema, so simplified to primary exit condition. Max adverse excursion condition also not directly expressible; stop_loss_pct of 2.0 captures the explicit stop level. Signal is LONG-only based on bullish anchoring exploitation.

source_rule_text: Exploit the anchoring bias where investors fixate on the 52-week high; when a stock trades near this anchor level, it becomes temporarily underpriced due to suppressed price response to bullish information, creating a momentum opportunity as the pricing error corrects with systematic upward movement.
